Operator-pasted DOI 10.5281/zenodo.19783312 abstract at ~10:55 JST clarifies that the preprint's 'Trans-stratum' is a TECHNICAL TERM excluding the 'Brouncker boundary class at ratio +1/4 (indicial double root 1/2)' which is named as 'a distinct stratum'. The (9,0,0,6,3) hit has a2/b1^2 = 9/36 = 1/4 -- it is a member of the Brouncker boundary class, NOT a Trans-stratum counterexample. Today's dispatch-script classifier labeled it 'Trans' under limit-type-only stratification (transcendental limit = Trans bucket); the preprint's stratum definition requires limit-type AND non-resonant indicial. CLASSIFIER MISMATCH, not falsification. The b1=6 sweep is therefore re-read as a SUCCESSFUL CROSS-RUN REPRODUCTION of the +1/4 Brouncker boundary class membership of (9,0,0,6,3).
